sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 01 of 2021 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.26 2. For orders on CMA No03/2021 3. For hearing of main case 14.01.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, advocate for the applicant Mr. Muhammad Masood Khan, advocate for respondent Pursuant to order dated 06.10.2025 learned counsel for respondent states that it may be in the interest of justice and revenue to set aside the impugned order and remand the matter back to the learned Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law expeditiously, preferably within 90 days. Learned counsel further seeks that pending the aforesaid no coercive action be taken against the respondent arising here from. Learned counsel for the applicant articulates no cavil in such regard and states that the reference application may be disposed off in the said terms. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
